title: Bee guidance: per-cell verify is scoped; the full verify runs at baseline, close, and merge only
action: Per verify-scoping D2. Codify the two-tier verify strategy, host-generically, in the two skills that teach it. In skills/bee-planning/SKILL.md, at the point where cell verify commands are authored: each cell's `verify` is the NARROWEST honest scoped check covering its change — the specific test file(s)/pattern for the touched area (in bee's own repo: `node scripts/run_verify.mjs --only <suite>` or the direct test file), never the full configured verify by default; the full suite is owed at exactly three moments — session baseline before first claim, feature close/session finish, worktree merge. In skills/bee-executing/SKILL.md, in the cap-evidence section: cap evidence is the CELL's scoped verify passing; a worker never runs the full configured verify per cap, and the orchestrator's done-report re-run uses the same scoped command — the full run belongs to close. Keep both edits short (a paragraph or a few lines each), matching each file's existing voice and structure; do not restructure the skills. Then re-render the managed skill mirrors so scripts/test_skill_render.mjs stays byte-identical, and regenerate the release manifest (scripts/release_manifest.mjs) so --check passes.
must_haves: bee-planning: scoped verify authoring rule + the three full-run moments, host-generic wording
bee-executing: cap evidence = cell's scoped verify; full verify belongs to close, not caps

trace.outcome: Codified verify-scoping D2 (scoped per-cell verify; full verify at baseline/close/merge only) into bee-planning and bee-executing SKILL.md, re-rendered all four managed mirrors (.claude, .agents, .claude-plugin, .codex-plugin skills + sidecars), and regenerated the release manifest.
trace.verify_output:
release_manifest --check: 499 file(s) match stored manifest
skill_render: 27 passed, 0 failed
